_notes: Hand-curated by reading the US Core Medication differential (2 elements) line by line against the R4 Medication snapshot. The differential has only 1 constrained element: Medication.code. R4 base: Medication.code min=0, max=1, binding=example:http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medication-codes, mustSupport=false. US Core adds: min=1, mustSupport=true, binding=extensible:http://cts.nlm.nih.gov/fhir/ValueSet/2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1010.4.

drifts.description: Cardinality changed: [0..1] -> [1..1]. Medication code becomes mandatory in US Core; R4 allows a Medication resource without a code.
MustSupport added in target (min becomes 1 -> conformance-critical).

Binding changed: R4 uses example strength to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medication-codes (general medication codes). US Core tightens to extensible strength with http://cts.nlm.nih.gov/fhir/ValueSet/2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1010.4 (US-specific medication clinical drug codes). Binding strength tightened: example -> extensible.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Medication.code in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
